entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop (Sorghum bicolor) whose production is limited by ergot disease

entities.name: ERGOT
entities.description: A fungal pathogen (Claviceps sorghi) that acts as a limiting factor for sorghum production

entities.name: RAINS
entities.description: Precipitation that promotes ergot infection when coinciding with flowering

entities.name: LOW TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Temperatures between 20-22 deg C that promote ergot infection

entities.name: GARLIC EXTRACT
entities.description: A crude extract used as a biocontrol agent to inhibit ergot

entities.name: ANDHRA PRADESH, INDIA
entities.description: The region where the efficacy of garlic extracts was tested between 1991-1993

entities.name: CONIDIAL GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of spore germination in ergot, which is inhibited by garlic extract

entities.name: GREENHOUSE CONDITIONS
entities.description: Controlled environment where garlic extract provided 98-100% ergot control

entities.name: FIELD CONDITIONS
entities.description: Natural environment where garlic extract provided approximately 90% ergot control

entities.name: POST-RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The period from February to March where garlic extract was effective

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The period during which garlic extract sprays were less effective

entities.name: POLLEN GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of pollen growth, which is not affected by garlic extract at concentrations of 12% and below

entities.name: HUMANS
entities.description: Species to which garlic extract is non-toxic

entities.name: BIOCONTROL AGENT
entities.description: A biological agent used for the environment friendly control of diseases

is non-toxic to at concentrations of 12% and below
